```{r}
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
grass_desc <- tibble::tribble(
  ~palette, ~use, ~description,
  "aspect", "General", "Aspect-oriented gray colors",
  "aspectcolr", "General", "Aspect-oriented rainbow colors",
  "bcyr", "General", "Blue through cyan and yellow to red",
  "bgyr", "General", "Blue through green and yellow to red",
  "blues", "General", "White to blue",
  "byg", "General", "Blue through yellow to green",
  "byr", "General", "Blue through yellow to red",
  "celsius", "General", "Blue to red for Celsius temperatures",
  "corine", "Land cover", "EU Corine land cover colors",
  "curvature", "General", "Terrain curvature colors",
  "differences", "General", "Difference-oriented colors",
  "elevation", "Topography", "Relative raster values mapped to elevation colors",
  "etopo2", "Topography", "ETOPO2 worldwide bathymetry and topography colors",
  "evi", "Natural", "Enhanced Vegetation Index colors",
  "fahrenheit", "Temperature", "Blue to red for Fahrenheit temperatures",
  "forest_cover", "Natural", "Percentage of forest cover",
  "gdd", "Natural", "Accumulated growing degree days",
  "grass", "General", "Perceptually uniform GRASS GIS green",
  "greens", "General", "White to green",
  "grey", "General", "Grayscale",
  "grey.eq", "General", "Histogram-equalized grayscale",
  "grey.log", "General", "Log-transformed grayscale",
  "grey1.0", "General", "Grayscale for raster values between 0.0 and 1.0",
  "grey255", "General", "Grayscale for raster values between 0 and 255",
  "gyr", "General", "Green through yellow to red",
  "haxby", "Topography", "Relative colors for bathymetry or topography",
  "inferno", "General", "Inferno perceptually uniform sequential color table",
  "kelvin", "Temperature", "Blue to red for temperatures in Kelvin",
  "magma", "General", "Magma perceptually uniform sequential color table",
  "ndvi", "Natural", "Normalized Difference Vegetation Index colors",
  "ndwi", "Natural", "Normalized Difference Water Index colors",
  "nlcd", "Land cover", "US National Land Cover Dataset colors",
  "oranges", "General", "White to orange",
  "plasma", "General", "Plasma perceptually uniform sequential color table",
  "population", "Human", "Human population classification breaks",
  "population_dens", "Human", "Human population density classification breaks",
  "precipitation", "Climate", "Precipitation color table, 0 to 2000 mm",
  "precipitation_daily", "Climate", "Daily precipitation color table, 0 to 1000 mm",
  "precipitation_monthly", "Climate", "Monthly precipitation color table, 0 to 1000 mm",
  "rainbow", "General", "Rainbow color table",
  "ramp", "General", "Color ramp",
  "random", "General", "Random color table",
  "reds", "General", "White to red",
  "roygbiv", "General", NA,
  "rstcurv", "General", "Terrain curvature from r.resamp.rst",
  "ryb", "General", "Red through yellow to blue",
  "ryg", "General", "Red through yellow to green",
  "sepia", "General", "Yellowish-brown to white",
  "slope", "General", "`r.slope.aspect`-style slope colors for raster values from 0 to 90",
  "soilmoisture", "Natural", "Soil moisture color table, 0.0 to 1.0",
  "srtm", "Topography", "Shuttle Radar Topography Mission elevation colors",
  "srtm_plus", "Topography", "Shuttle Radar Topography Mission elevation colors with seafloor colors",
  "terrain", "Topography", "Global elevation color table from -11000 to +8850 m",
  "viridis", "General", "Viridis perceptually uniform sequential color table",
  "water", "Natural", "Water depth",
  "wave", "General", "Color wave"
)
initscipen <- getOption("scipen")
options(scipen = 15)
allpals <- tidyterra::grass_db |>
  group_by(pal) |>
  summarise(
    min_l = min(limit),
    max_l = max(limit)
  ) |>
  mutate(range = ifelse(is.na(min_l),
    NA,
    paste0(min_l, " to ", max_l)
  )) |>
  select(palette = pal, range)

options(scipen = initscipen)

final_tab <- grass_desc |>
  inner_join(allpals) |>
  mutate(palette = paste0("`", palette, "`"))
final_tab[is.na(final_tab)] <- ""

nms <- paste0("**", colnames(final_tab), "**")

knitr::kable(final_tab, col.names = nms)
```
